wiki.content: “Asheville” finds glaive reflecting on his complicated relationship with his hometown, dealing with past mistakes, ongoing urges, and his changing feelings toward the place. Reflecting on his past in the verses, he confronts himself in the chorus; the outro hears him flaunt his better self, notably shouting out his closest friends while putting his past behind him. glaive first previewed “Asheville' in Los Angeles on February 19, 2025, to celebrate the May It Never Falter tour’s America leg coming to an end; he previewed the song again in Asheville on April 19, 2025, at his they named the city after me charity show. The lead single for y'all was announced on July 3, 2025, after a social media stunt tricked hundreds into believing a fake pre-save link. Hours after the prank began, glaive took to his private Twitter to call it out and followed with an informal announcement. A week later, July 10, he formally announced the release date and cover. (made in Alaska) "Upon returning to my hometown, I had this new found confidence mixed with a bit of shame for making ‘Fuck this town’ because in the end it actually is quite a nice place. I picked this song to be the first single because 1: it's a crazy song and 2: i think it quite sums up a lot of the way I was feeling when I first started making this album." -glaive via yall.ong Read more on Last.fm.
